commit.message:
Fix shouldWriteFile to detect text-to-binary reclassification

When force-binary reclassifies a file from TextContent to BinaryContent,
the existing metadata file is a text content copy (not hash+size format).
Check if the file parses as metadata before comparing hashes — if it
doesn't, force a rewrite to replace the text copy with binary metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: Version control for large files. Git tracks metadata, rclone syncs content. No server required — works with any storage backend (Google Drive, USB drives, NAS). Diff-based sync, device-aware remotes, conflict resolution. Written in Haskell.

commit.message:
Add feedback loops to all 7 GTM skills

Each skill now has a dedicated feedback loop step that triggers on revisit:
- ICP Sharpener: recalibrate scoring from real conversion data
- Voice Builder: consistency scoring against recent posts
- Daily Debrief: seed prioritization, clustering, and performance tracking
- Idea Sourcer: track which ideas performed, weight future searches
- Post Repurposer: log post performance, surface winning patterns
- Outreach Packager: reply tracking, signal optimization across batches
- Meeting Prep Brief: post-call debrief, cumulative context per prospect

Skills now get sharper with use — not static templates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 3 bare `simp [resolveOffset]` calls from PR #687 to use explicit
`simp only` with specific lemmas. Also fix linter warning: replace
`simp only [letFun]` with `dsimp only [letFun]` (letFun contributes
only via dsimp).

commit.message:
fix: use BIRD_BIN env var to avoid Ubuntu system bird routing daemon

The Ubuntu runner has /usr/sbin/bird (BIRD routing daemon) which shadows
our @steipete/bird Twitter CLI. Set BIRD_BIN to the exact npm install
path after install and use it in the scraper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(rpc): chunked queryFilter to respect 50K block range limit

All public RPCs (PublicNode, Ankr, etc) reject eth_getLogs with range
>50K blocks. Our EVENTS_LOOKBACK spans ~63K+ blocks from deploy.
Added chunkedQueryFilter utility that splits large ranges into safe
45K-block chunks. Applied to all queryFilter calls: Agora, Airdrop,
Notary, Fortune history, Referral.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(cardapio): cache priority, ID mismatch, NaN-safe portions

BUG 1: resultado-cardapio now loads localStorage first (fresh POST data)
before falling back to DB cache, ensuring new preferences are reflected.

BUG 2: Added 12 missing foods to alimentos-taco.ts that existed in the
preferences UI but not in the TACO database (peru, proteina_vegetal,
grao_bico, lentilha, quinoa, batata_inglesa, inhame, cara,
macarrao_integral, uva, manteiga, oleo_girassol). Added proteina_vegetal
group to variation-engine category map.

BUG 3: Added calcularPorcaoSegura() with NaN/Infinity guards. Replaced
all inline portion calculations (gordura, proteina boost) to use safe
version. Added NaN guard to montarItem() as final safety net.

fix(ui): ion FormulaChip hover highlights periodic table and solubility table

FormulaChip was skipping dispatchHighlight for ion chips (if (!isIon)
guard). Now all chips dispatch: ions send { elements, ionId }, substances
send { elements, formula } as before.

SolubilityTable onHighlight handler gains ionId branch: cation ionId →
highlight row, anion ionId → highlight column. externalHighlight type
relaxed to { cation?, anion? } to support single-axis selection.

PeriodicTableHint already uses detail.elements, so ion element highlighting
works with no changes there.

commit.message:
Auto-discover experiments, uniform time budgets, new icon

- run.py no longer has a hardcoded registry; it discovers any .py
  file in simulation/experiments/ with a run() function and TITLE.
  Adding a new experiment requires zero edits to shared files.
- All experiments get the same kwargs (n_seeds, max_nodes, mem_bits,
  time_budget) and ignore what they don't need via **_.
- Uniform defaults: 60s quick, 300s full for every experiment.
- Title bar: ANTILOPE -> ANTILOOP.
- Icon: deer emoji from Windows Segoe UI Emoji font.

test(sync): add scenario tests for mgw:sync drift detection and auto-sync (#266)

Covers four scenarios from issue #255:
- No-drift baseline: GH updatedAt <= local mtime → no sync fires
- Stale auto-sync: GH updatedAt > local mtime → state refreshed, console.warn logged
- Comment delta drift: current count > stored count → flagged in report
- PR merged but stage=pr-created → pipeline_stage transitions to done

28 tests across 5 describe blocks (plus integration sync report verification).
Uses mock-github.cjs for gh CLI interception and fs.utimesSync for mtime control.

Also includes vitest infrastructure (vitest.config.js, test/setup.js,
test/loadFixture.js, lib/mock-github.cjs, test/fixtures/github/) carried
forward from PR #265 dependency.

  questionnaire trigger po parse_campaign_request

Flow:
1. User pisze "stwórz kampanię dla dre"
2. Bot widzi brak budżetu i linku → pyta o nie
3. User odpowiada "50 zł, https://dre.pl"
4. Bot merguje odpowiedź z pending params → tworzy kampanię

commit.message:
fix(ext/node): make TTY stdout/stderr indestructible (#32530)

## Summary

- Libraries like `mute-stream` (used by `@inquirer/prompts`) call
`destroy()` or `end()` on `process.stdout`, which previously closed the
underlying Deno resource (rid 1/2). This broke subsequent `console.log`
calls with `BadResource: Bad resource ID` because `op_print` looks up
the resource in the table.
- Match Node.js behavior where stdio streams are indestructible:
- Override `_destroy` with a dummy that calls `_undestroy()` to revert
stream state without closing the handle (same as Node.js's
`dummyDestroy` in `is_main_thread.js`)
- Override `_handle._onClose` to prevent the `end()` -> `_final` ->
`shutdown` -> `_onClose` path from closing the resource

Closes #32513

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: security audit — invitation system vulnerabilities

- CRITICAL: add isAdminOfTeam() guard to DELETE/resend invitation routes
  (any team member could previously cancel or regenerate invite links)
- CRITICAL: split TeamInvitation::isValid() — remove side effects (auto-delete
  + user cleanup); add cleanupIfExpired() for explicit use in CleanupJob only
- HIGH: decline route now requires auth and email ownership check
  (unauthenticated users could previously delete any invite by knowing UUID)
- HIGH: invite creation now restricted to owner/admin only (developer excluded)
  — applies to POST /settings/team/invite and invite/data GET endpoint
- HIGH: implement admin resend invitation (was a no-op stub)
- MEDIUM: remove inviter_email from public GET /auth/invitations/{uuid} response
- MEDIUM: remove dead SettingsController::inviteMember (wrong role validation,
  not registered in any route)

Add theme-based Network Spotlight with rotation

Replace generic AI summary with 5 computed themes (founders at funded
companies, career movers, dormant gold, company stage distribution,
investor overlap). Each theme is scored by interestingness and the
component supports cycling through themes via "New insight" button

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix auth race condition causing infinite loading on page reload and sign-in

Rewrote AuthContext to use onAuthStateChange as the sole source of truth
instead of racing getSession() with INITIAL_SESSION event. Key changes:
- Remove init()/getSession() — use INITIAL_SESSION event instead
- Set loading=true synchronously in signIn() before API call
- Set loading=true on SIGNED_IN event before fetching profile
- Skip profile refetch on TOKEN_REFRESHED (just update session)
- Add 15s safety timeout to prevent infinite loading edge cases
- Use useRef for mount tracking instead of closure variable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Switch resume_link to raw.githubusercontent.com (bypasses Pages serving)
